prompt:
I picked up the tool-lending-shed ledger crate from a volunteer who moved away, and cargo won't have it. Six errors, four different flavours — here's the headline of each (the full output with all the compiler's suggestions is enormous):

  error[E0106]: missing lifetime specifier
    --> src/lib.rs:53:40  (pub fn pick_label(a: &str, b: &str) -> &str)
  error[E0308]: mismatched types
    --> src/lib.rs:64:19  — card.push_str(tool.name): expected `&str`, found `String`
  error[E0308]: mismatched types
    --> src/lib.rs:66:19  — card.push_str(tool.category.clone()): expected `&str`, found `String`
  error[E0382]: borrow of moved value: `tool`
    --> src/lib.rs:36:64  — value moved into inventory.push, then borrowed for the intake log line
  error[E0502]: cannot borrow `*members` as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable
    --> src/lib.rs:46:9
  error[E0502]: cannot borrow `*loans` as mutable because it is also borrowed as immutable
    --> src/lib.rs:78:13
  error: could not compile `rs-toolshed` (lib) due to 6 previous errors

A plea before you start slapping .clone() on everything the compiler frowns at: the doc comments say what each function promises (intake log lines in donation order, culled loans reported in order and actually removed, the notice line, all of it) and tests/toolshed.rs holds those promises — the behavior must come out the other side intact. `cargo test --offline` green is done; tests/, Cargo.toml and .gitignore stay untouched.
notes: Borrow-checker triage x5 (six diagnostics): move-after-use in the donation loop (E0382), immutable-then-mutable overlap in notice_line (E0502), returned reference needing an explicit lifetime in pick_label (E0106), String vs &str mismatches x2 in shelf_card (E0308), and remove-during-iteration in cull_overdue (E0502). Probed on rustc 1.96.0: all six diagnostics surface in a single cargo pass — no chain reveals. The cull test includes two ADJACENT overdue loans so an index-walking fix that skips after remove() fails; reference fix uses retain. notice_line's doc comment pins that the busiest member is computed over the pre-signup roster (signups join with zero loans, so any compiling fix agrees on the tested data).
